title: Foreign body sensation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports foreign-body sensation, grittiness, red eye, watering, and mechanical or chemical context.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

presenting_history.text: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports foreign-body or contact-lens context plus pain, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

exact_section.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports visual loss, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, and post-traumatic warning prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

red_flags.text: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.
red_flags.rationale: Supports chemical, penetrating, embedded-foreign-body, laceration, and severe-abrasion warning prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table

exact_section.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals

red_flags.text: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.
red_flags.rationale: Supports severe eye pain and visual-loss warning context.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table

exact_section.heading: Annex 4 — visual loss and other urgent presentations

referral.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports inability-to-open-eye, worsening, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation without automatic urgency.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

referral.text: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports fluorescein and assessed corneal or foreign-body fields without a removal procedure.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports visual review, trauma, contact-lens, foreign-body-sensation, and assessed red-eye fields.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

source_limitations: Laterality, eyelid eversion, irrigation, foreign-body removal, broad supportive care, fixed same-day or 24-hour follow-up, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer a retained foreign body, abrasion, keratitis, chemical injury, or penetrating injury or generate drops, antibiotics, irrigation, removal, or referral advice.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
